summary: Re-verified the two bug fixes in /app/php-version/setup-check.php (POST handler ordering + JSON-blob allowlist) flagged by iteration_3. All 19 tests in test_public_domain_hygiene.py now PASS (1 unrelated checkout test skipped because gateway in test mode). Strengthened the suite with stricter assertions: HTTP 302 (not 200) on fix POST, exact leak-row count == 3 (excludes seo_health_probe_cache), data-testid hooks present, cleaned=3 in redirect Location, hygiene-clean-state element shown after fix. Added a dedicated regression test verifying JSON-blob rows are excluded from the leak table even when their value contains preview.emergentagent.com.
critical_code_review_comments: URL-key allowlist is duplicated in setup-check.php (line 97-98) and includes/settings.php setting_get() (line 18-44). Inline comment already warns about this. Consider extracting into a shared constant URL_SETTING_KEYS in includes/settings.php and importing from setup-check.php to prevent drift.

Cleanup regex uses unanchored https?://[^/\s"\']*\.preview\.emergentagent\.com — solid for the documented hostnames, but if Emergent ever issues a hostname like xxx.preview.emergentagent.com.cdn.cf (i.e. a sub-host with extra path-y suffix on the host part), the character class [^/\s"\'] would still match correctly. Low risk; just worth a comment.

POST fix handler swallows all Throwable exceptions silently (line 124) — a DB error during cleanup would still produce a 302 with cleaned=0 and no operator-visible feedback. Consider logging via error_log() for debuggability.

success_rate.backend: 100% (19/19 tests passed, 1 skipped due to test-mode gateway)

seed_data_creation: Re-seeded leaky settings rows before running tests: main_url='https://robot-rules.preview.emergentagent.com', site_domain_url='https://robot-rules.preview.emergentagent.com', company_logo='https://robot-rules.preview.emergentagent.com/uploads/company/logo-356b9f03.png', plus seo_health_probe_cache JSON blob containing embedded preview URLs. After fix execution: main_url='', site_domain_url='', company_logo='/uploads/company/logo-356b9f03.png', seo_health_probe_cache untouched (correctly excluded).
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_public_domain_hygiene.py now has 20 tests. The setup-check hygiene test will need leaky DB rows re-seeded before each run because the test executes the fix and clears them. Use: mysql -uroot ucode_store -e "UPDATE settings SET v='https://robot-rules.preview.emergentagent.com' WHERE k IN ('main_url','site_domain_url'); UPDATE settings SET v='https://robot-rules.preview.emergentagent.com/uploads/company/logo-356b9f03.png' WHERE k='company_logo'". The checkout-flow test is intermittently skipped when the payment gateway is in test mode and the form doesn't post a redirect — this is expected and unrelated to the domain-hygiene work.
rca of the issue: Both bugs documented in iteration_3 are now fully resolved. (1) POST handler relocated above include admin-shell.php at line ~103, so the header('Location:...') call fires before any output is sent — confirmed by HTTP 302 with cleaned=3 in Location header. (2) Both detection SELECT and fix loop now use the same $urlSettingKeys allowlist (['main_url','site_domain_url','company_logo','site_url','public_url','base_url']), so JSON-blob rows like seo_health_probe_cache (whose v happens to contain preview URLs inside a JSON string) are never selected as candidates — confirmed by exact leak-row count == 3, with seo_health_probe_cache absent from the table. The unanchored regex change is belt-and-suspenders for any non-URL-key edge case that might slip through in the future.
